candidates.stmt:
Dear Resident,

I’m Oscar James Wood, your Reform UK candidate for Belfairs Ward. I am a 43-year-old married father of three children, I am completely new to politics having never been involved with any party before. My family members own a successful award winning small business located on Eastwood Old Road, I have lived in the Southend area for over 10 years and Belfairs Ward is close to my heart.

My 5 main local priorities will be:

1: Improving the state of the pavement surfaces.
2: Safer roads without potholes.
3: Protecting the green belt. 
4: Champion our local small businesses.
5: Protect existing Bus routes from closure.

I have experienced the same struggles as many local families, including post-Covid employment challenges, concerns about the quality of education, lawless crime, and the increasing cost of living.

Many older residents remember more stable times, while my generation faces growing debt, stagnant wages, and a lack of local investment, often leaving people feeling compelled to look outside the UK for a better quality of life. I love our country and am passionate about preserving it before it declines further.

With this in mind, I joined Reform UK to challenge the local financial waste (such as the Better Queensway project) and the incompetence that exists within the Labour, Lib-Dem & Independents administration of Southend City Council. Each year, council tax rises while the standard of local services and infrastructure declines, with too little accountability.

A safe and stable community is essential for empowering local businesses and keeping jobs in the area. Small businesses in Belfairs are struggling, through my family I can relate to the challenges they face, small local retail businesses are a lifeline for many residents who may not be able to travel into the city centre. Against the odds of a failing economy and repeated road closures on the Fairway, these shops are working hard simply to stay afloat. Our pubs and churches are also

Lauren Ekins is the Green Party candidate for Belfairs Ward.

A former Police Officer, Lauren is currently a local schoolteacher. She is a volunteer for a local youth group and is a regular volunteer in local community cleanup events.

Lauren is a key member of the local Green Party and is involved in several of our important community campaigns.

As the upcoming local council elections draw near, I am honoured to once again stand in Belfairs ward as your non-aligned independent candidate.

At the last election, I was proud to receive the support of 420 residents across Belfairs. That level of support meant a great deal to me and showed that many people in our community are looking for strong, independent representation. This year, I am building on that and hoping to earn the support of even more residents.

First and foremost, I believe in the immense potential of our community. Belfairs is not just a place we inhabit; it's our home, where our families grow, our businesses thrive, our futures unfold and memories are made. I am deeply committed to preserving and enhancing the unique charm and vitality of our area.

Throughout my career in various health and social care roles and settings, ranging from addiction services to homelessness and mental health, I have witnessed first-hand the importance of being able to access services to support our needs, and ensuring that our voices are heard. Every individual in Belfairs deserves to be seen, heard, and represented, regardless of their background, beliefs, or circumstances. As a non-aligned Independent candidate, I am dedicated to representing residents based on their needs and concerns, free from the influence of any political party agenda. I am committed to fostering an inclusive and participatory democracy where every resident’s concerns and aspirations are valued and addressed.

Furthermore, I am passionate about driving positive change and progress in our community. From ensuring access to quality education and healthcare to promoting infrastructure improvements, I am determined to work collaboratively with fellow councillors, community organisations, and residents to tackle the challenges we face and seize the opportunities before us.

Additionally, I am deeply committed to transparency, accountability, and integrity in governance. As your elected represent

Alan served as a Councillor in Leigh ward for many years, and prided himself on looking at the big picture as well as serving his ward.

He also served as a county councillor and an East of England regional member.

His achievements include working with others to stop the demolition of Old Leigh to build a major road, stop London sending waste to Essex and treat it within London instead, and upgrade the Felixstowe to Nuneaton railway line to take thousands of lorries off the A12.

Alan is well-known within Belfairs having served many of its residents as a dentist over many years.
I am pleased to be standing in my local area as a candidate. Please contact me with anything and I will try to get back to you.

Dear Resident,

You will shortly be casting your vote for the Local Southend-on-Sea City Council elections taking place on 7th May 2026.

I am standing to deliver real results for Blenheim Park Ward — focused on the everyday issues that matter most to you and your family.

My approach is simple: listen to residents, take action, and hold the Council to account. Whether it’s safer streets, reliable services, or protecting opportunities for local families, I am committed to being a strong, active voice for our community.

For months, I have been working across the ward to tackle local concerns — from potholes, pavements, parking and verge maintenance to resolving waste collection issues with SUEZ. I continue to report fly-tipping and graffiti to keep our area clean, and have raised concerns about speeding and dangerous parking with local police.

I’m a local resident and a software engineer who leads a team, and in my spare time I enjoy art, reading, walks by the sea, and volunteering in our community — from litter picks along Prittle Brook Greenway to supporting events like the Leigh Regatta and Poppy Appeal.

At the Council, I have been challenging the Labour administration on its budget, which relies on reserves without a clear long-term plan. Residents deserve better value and more efficient services.

I remain active locally through street surgeries, walkabouts, and community litter picks, including our upcoming Blenheim Park clean-up. If elected, I will continue working hard for our community.

Please lend me your support when you cast your vote.

Yours sincerely,

Zhanelya Subebayeva

You can visit my Facebook page, https://facebook.com/zhanelya.subebayeva.blenheim or contact me via email at zhanelya2026@gmail.com should you need any help with a local issue.

A political candidate for Blenheim Park Ward. Promoted by Meg Davidson on behalf of Zhanelya Subebayeva. Both at Iveagh Hall, 67 Leigh Road, Leigh-on-Sea, SS9 1JW

AJ has lived in Southend all his life, apart from a brief departure to study Applied & Environmental Geology in Leicester. He is a local healthcare professional, working in pharmacy and medication safety.

As a proud member of the LGBTIQA+ community, AJ is an outspoken advocate for equal rights across all marginalised communities.

AJ is passionate about holding governments and news outlets to account for their role in climate change and has taken part in grassroots campaigning with the Climate Genocide Act Now group. AJ says his aim is to leave a positive impact on the world.

AJ has stood for Blenheim Park since 2021.

I had the honour and privilege to have served you until last May as your ‘Truly’ non-aligned Independent councillor.

During my time in the Army, I was awarded the Meritorious Service Medal (MSM) in recognition of ‘going beyond the call of duty,’ and I believe that during the past years that I served and represented you, the residents, to that same standard. My attendance at meetings was exemplary. This, I believe, demonstrated my full commitment to the role and the prioritising of my time so that the residents were represented at every meeting. The statistics are available at https://democracy.southend.gov.uk

During my time serving you as your councillor it only reinforced my belief that the local elections are about local issues, and local people, and I firmly believe that they should be free of party allegiance with all the Councillors working together for and on behalf of the residents we represent.

I am standing again for election as a ‘truly’ non-aligned Independent Councillor, (not part of a political party or group), to enable me to continue to serve YOU without the constraints of national party guidelines or local party policies, which allows me to continue to represent ALL the residents of Blenheim Park Ward, irrespective of your political views.

I initially stood for election because I felt politically homeless in the political climate, and I was disillusioned with our local politicians and the bureaucrats treating us with contempt and not listening or respecting our views. I wanted real democracy and felt it was time for me as an ordinary local resident who lives locally and shares your daily frustrations, to do something about the issues we encounter and to be able to make a difference.

I believe I made a difference and that is why I’m standing again for re-election in Blenheim Park Ward.

When I was elected, I promised:

To ensure the views and the interests of the local people are represented within the council  To promote the interests of local p

Greetings Blenheim Park Ward. I'm Lynn, a mother of 5 who has lived in Southend for 26 years. I'm standing for The Heritage Party as I believe we can protect our children and build a stronger, free nation. Our City has suffered under previous parties. You can make a difference by voting for The Heritage Party.

I am very passionate about keeping children safe, and oppose the RSE curriculums in schools and the right for parents to remove them from such classes. The sexualisation of children is not limited to the classroom, and I aim to ensure an end to the sexualisation and grooming not only in our schools but in spaces too.

Children and sexuality do not belong in the same sentence and they should not be asked about this by their teachers, or exposed to inappropriate lesson content, or books in our children’s libraries that damage their innocence or confuses them about their gender. All parents should be outraged about the schools bill. Restore our parental rights! Our children belong to us not the State.

All of Southend’s Heritage Party candidates understand, that to begin with councillors need to first go back to basics and ensure that our City runs efficiently, serving the basic needs of the people, before it accepts incentives and schemes from central government, (of which it does not legally have to! Such as the Southend SMART city scheme — part of agenda 2030, which means loss of your freedoms, control, tracking and risks to your health and safety.) We understand that the majority of Southend dislike the new bin system, which we would seek to reverse and any other such changes should be democratically put to those who pay council tax. Changes should be made based on what is best for our City and its residents, rather than ticking boxes of goals from ‘Net Zero’ climate scam nonsense, of which the council and many others have no grounds to be spending money on, as they can not prove a ‘climate emergency’ on scientific grounds.

We are pretty sure as a bare mini

Kay Mitchell has lived in Southend for more than 30 years and brought up her family here. Having worked across support services most of her working life Kay has a clear understanding of social and emotional health, as well as systems of support. She has experience of multi agency working and managing case work. As a former councillor and cabinet member she is familiar with council processes and procedures. Kay is an activist for the NHS and preserving resident support services, having seen the impact of cuts over many years across the city. Kay has been involved in projects with residents including getting road and footway repairs done, pushing for Chalkwell access ramp to be reinstated, and attending water quality meetings. Kay regularly takes residents concerns on health, investment in infrastructure, transport and regreening the city to council meetings, while standing up for staff and students at the university and supporting protection of our green belt. Kay is a co-founder of a family friendly multi cultural festival celebrating our performers, art, culture and community, that also promotes local business. Kay has demonstrated that she is  able to represent residents as a Green Party candidate working directly with residents on their priorities and continuing to improve outcomes in Chalkwell, applying strong values in social and environmental justice, and a good quality of life for all.

Chalkwell is one of Southend's jewels in the crown with our beautiful beachfront, excellent local park and friendly neighbourhoods.

I bring with me vast experience as a procurement professional of 19 years, spanning the public and charity sectors. I started my career at Southend City (then Borough) Council before working within the NHS and then the City of London Corporation. I currently work for a research charity in London supporting cutting-edge scientific discovery. My career has been dedicated to serving the public interest, which goes to the core of why I have decided to stand.

Through my work, I have witnessed first-hand the importance of local democracy, efficient public services, and robust support systems for vulnerable members of our community.

Furthermore, my experience in procurement has instilled in me a strong sense of integrity, business ethics and a drive to ensure value for money. I understand the importance of allocating resources wisely and making data-based decisions. I will work diligently to ensure that our Council’s budget is utilised effectively and to address the needs of our community.

Above all, I am deeply invested in the future of our community and am dedicated to improving the outcomes for all residents from East to West, North to South.

With your support, I am eager to roll up my sleeves and work tirelessly to build a brighter future for all of our families and generations to come.

Hi, my name’s James Vessey-Miller, and I’m pleased to be standing for election to Southend City Council for the Green Party.

This isn’t my first time standing for the Greens. I’ve been a candidate in every election in Southend since 2018, and for nearly a decade I’ve also been attending council and community meetings, helping residents and local groups get answers on important local issues, and doing my bit as one of the city’s many active citizens.

Some people will know me from that local work. Others might know me from my background in business management, including managing hospitality venues and public events here in Southend. That’s given me a practical understanding of what local businesses need, and of how important our tourism, arts, music, and nightlife are to the life of our city.

I now work in Westminster for the Green Party, and I’m very open about that. I mention it because it’s given me valuable experience and a close view of how politics and public administration work in practice, including in places where Greens are already running councils and getting on with the job. But my roots, my record, and my reputation were built here in Southend long before that, and my focus remains firmly on this city and the people who live here.

My social media also gives a snapshot of that track record, and of the many issues I’ve worked on over the years for residents, local groups, and our wider community, including alongside the Green city councillors we already have here in Southend.

I’m genuinely impressed by the Green candidates we’ve got standing across Southend in these elections. We’ve got a strong group of thoughtful, hardworking people, and I’d be very pleased to see more of them elected. I’m not only asking for support for my own candidacy. I’m also working hard to help get our other Green candidates elected across the city too.

For me, local politics should be about making life better for people here, and looking after the place we all call home. Tha

On 7 May, I will be standing for re-election in #KursaalWard and asking residents for another term as their councillor.

At this election I will be standing on my record of championing our community and its needs, and a vision for the future for our community. These are my top five priorities for #KursaalWard.

🏛️To save the Kursaal and get it reopened for everyone to enjoy.

The recent news of a new leaseholder at the Kursaal is a fantastic step forward towards saving this historic building, but there is still a lot of work to do to see it reopened. If re-elected, I will continue to prioritise the Kursaal, and will finish the job.

🏘️To get proper controls and limits on the spread of HMOs.

HMOs have a place in the housing ecosystem, but the concentration of them in #KursaalWard is harming our community. If re-elected, I will make sure that the local plan includes the strong policies we need to tackle the spread of HMOs.

🛣️To continue investing in fixing our roads and pavements.

Decades of neglect have left our roads and pavements in a shocking and dangerous state. Last year our administration invested £13m in repairs, and this year we're investing another £13m. If re-elected, I will make sure Kursaal gets its fair share of that money.

📢To continue to be a constant champion for our community.

No one has more passionately celebrated or stood up for our community. There are certainly problems in #KursaalWard, but I know what a wonderful place this can be to live, work and play. If re-elected, I will never stop fighting to make our community the best it can be.

🗣️To always be available and accessible to Kursaal residents.

In my time as #KursaalWard councillor, I have consistently been one of the most accessible and transparent councillors on the Council. If re-elected, I will continue this, holding regular surgeries and being available to help residents with local issues.

These are not all of my aspirations and ambitions for #KursaalWard, but merely a top five.

Billy attended school in Southend and maintained links with the area his entire life. He has lived in Southend with his wife and grown-up step-children (who attended local publicly-funded schools) for the last 10 years, and runs a small family business based in Southend, which often involves working in St Luke's ward. Billy is an active supporter of a number of local charities, including HARP, the Southend Hospitals Charity and the Essex Wildlife Trust.

One of Billy's great pleasures is his wildlife garden at home that welcomes breeding frogs and newts each year, and this year he has acquired an allotment for the first time.

He has been a supporter of Southend United for many years: getting to games when other commitments allow.

He is proud to support the role the Liberal Democrats currently play on Southend Council, planting more trees and looking to increase bio-diversity, supporting our local businesses, and looking after our money responsibly.

He is keen to ensure that St Luke's continues to receive its fair share of investment, and will work with local residents, and especially Project Southchurch, to promote the economy and culture in the area and seek to maximise its potential.

Hi Kursaal Ward, 

I have lived and worked in the southend area for most of my life, As a hairdresser I actively talk to residents from all backgrounds and walks of life and feel I have a good grasp of what the people really want. 

My main concern for this area is the amount of cell towers being erected, without informing the residents.  This is part of the basic infrastructure for the plan of southend becoming a smart city, which is set to be complete by 2050. Of which residents also have had no voice in the decision to do so. No debate has been opened on how this will affect residents' lives, and health.  There have been no risk assessments carried out, and many studies showing high risks of cancer and other health related issues, not to mention the impact they may have on housing prices in the future. 

I feel we need to prioritize cleaning up the city visually and in regards to the violent crimes that are unmet by our police. 

I chose to run under the Heritage party as they oppose 15 minute cities and low traffic neighborhoods. And I share their belief in ‘common sense thinking’ 

We need to stand together to invoke change.  I need your vote, to be the voice for you, the people.

Anita Forde is the Green Party candidate for Leigh Ward.

Anita has lived in Southend all her life and has been part of the Leigh community for over 40 years. She knows the area well, having gone to school locally and built her working life here.

She previously ran a hairdressing business in Leigh for 18 years, training apprentices and supporting local initiatives, including helping to establish the Leigh Christmas Lights as part of the original Leigh Traders’ Association.

After raising two sons as a single parent, Anita retrained as a History teacher. She went on to spend 15 years in secondary education, gaining a Master’s in Education and standing up for staff and students through her work with the NASUWT, including serving as South Essex President.

Today, Anita runs a hairdressers once again on London Road and remains deeply involved in the community. She has supported a wide range of local events and organisations, including the Leigh Regatta, youth projects, and the Winter Night Shelter.

Elected twice to Leigh Town Council, Anita has worked to give young people a stronger voice, setting up a monthly Youth Forum and supporting local youth clubs in partnership with the YMCA.

She is now working closely with local Green councillors and is standing to bring her experience, energy and commitment to the city council.

Sarah-Ann Jarine is the Green Party candidate for Milton Ward.

Sarah-Ann has lived and worked in Southend for the past 10 years. Passionate about community and social equality, she is on the board of trustees at the Milton Community Partnership and works at a Southend-based charity.

A Mum of two school kids, she is concerned about air pollution and the world we are leaving to the next generation and wants to see a vote for real change.

1 Save our Green Belt
2 sensible spending of your Council Taz
3 Regenerate Southend

Robert 'Bob' Howes is a lifelong Southend resident.

As well as being a committed Liberal Democrat, and previously Liberal for many years, he is an active member of the Southend & District Pensioners Campaign.

Away from politics he is a big fan of playing and listening to classical music and jazz and is a keen tennis player.

Milton ward, it would be an honor to stand up for you and to serve you. It is time for councils to remind themselves of their obligations towards the public; no decision should be made without a clear transparent legitimate public consultation, to us the people.

Having been a Southend resident for 17 years, I have long loved this city and hate to see the state it has fallen into. We desperately need change from the old parties which have repeatedly let us down! 

As a home educator, and someone who runs multiple community projects, such as the people's health alliance hub, I am very in touch with the residents' issues, concerns, wants and needs. Many of which I experience daily myself. 

My main priority is to stop southend from becoming a ‘15 minute city’.  These models that have been rolled out by other councils, in spite of residents voting against them, they take away our freedoms, in many different ways and levels and are completely ‘miss sold’ to the public.  And oppose the related issues linked to agenda 2030.  As well as:  Better management of residents housing issues including mold, for both private and council tenants.  Stop the spilling of sewage in the sea.  Transparency in spending on emergency services, and spending more money on training police officers, that will actually come to reported scenes, and making sure our police staff spend less time and money on monitoring twitter and political correctness and more time dealing with physical assaults and real crime.  Allow all local businesses to thrive, not only the high street but hamlet court road and all those in betweens should be able to benefit from any schemes that are introduced. Free use of the public library, particularly for the home education community, And the removal of inappropriate materials for children from it.  Stop rampant development, and stop over riding residents' opposition to do so, protect our historical sites and listed buildings.  Clean up our city, force landlords to provide

I’m standing as your Labour and Co-operative Party candidate in the May 2026 local elections because I believe our community deserves a council that works with people, not just for them.
I moved to the UK 22 years ago and to Southend in 2022, where my family and I now call home. My children attend local schools, so I understand firsthand the importance of strong public services, safe neighbourhoods, and opportunities for young people.
Professionally, I work as an IT consultant, bringing problem-solving skills and a practical approach to delivering results. Alongside this, I’ve been actively involved in our community—raising funds for Havens Hospices, supporting local initiatives, and distributing free books to our local library to encourage reading and learning.
I believe in being proactive and stepping up where help is needed. Whether it’s supporting local causes or working with residents to solve issues, I am committed to making a positive difference.
As a Labour and Co-operative candidate, I stand for fairness, community, and working together. If elected, I will:
•	Be a visible, approachable, and accountable local councillor
Most importantly, I will listen to residents, take action, and stand up for our community.
I would be honoured to have your support.

RJ grew up in South Essex and works in Brentwood. As a local Healthcare Professional with over a decade of frontline service, they know how vital our public services like the NHS are. They have seen firsthand the impact of years of underfunding these critical services have taken; a decline that has accelerated since the pandemic.

RJ is a proactive member of the Green Party, contributing to the Party's Policy Development Groups on topics including Drug Reform, LGBTIQA+ rights, and Immigration and Asylum. RJ is an active volunteer in the Green Party's campaigning across South Essex, including taking a key role in the local election wins for the Greens in Southend-on-Sea.

In their own time, RJ supports Essex's vibrant grassroots music scene; a lifelong love of music, literature, and art which began at Brentwood's Hermit Youth Club, a sorely missed local facility for our young people.

Living in and raising her family in Shoeburyness over the past 40 years, as well as practicing as a social worker and youth worker here, Tricia believes she can understand the many issues residents face.  She feels it is important to have a local representative who understands these issues, and is able to listen and respond appropriately.

Tricia’s experience in social care and youth work as well her extensive involvement with the voluntary sector, and within commissioning, have given her a real insight into a wide range of council matters.  She brings her experience as a senior manager and former CEO of a children’s charity and has been able to utilise these skills and experiences enhance her decision making and enable her to represent her residents effectively.

Tricia is a keen activist, starting up a campaign to keep the Shoeburyness Ambulance station fully operational, campaigning with local residents to protect our trees and biodiversity and to improve bus services.

She has previously been an elected councillor, sitting in a number of committees and chairing the People Scrutiny committee, facilitating special projects, including  one around improving outcomes for children with SEND and their families.

Tricia is a member of the East Beach Resident's Association as well as the Shoebury Society and local U3A and has a good understanding of the issues facing Shoeburyness residents.  She states: 'I know the deep problems that this ward faces, but also the many opportunities it offers. I want to see those opportunities available to all, and a community which values and encourages everyone”.

A vote for Tricia Cowdrey would gain you a Green Party  councillor who is hard working and passionate about Shoeburyness and its people.

If you want real change then stand in your local election and make it happen. 
The power is with the people not the government 
Time we worked together to use that power and save the future for our children 
Stop believing the lies the BBC tell you and fight back against the peadophilla that has been allowed in our country. 
I got into this for the children’s future because I am disgusted at what’s happening, all I saw was them being let down. 
When you don’t vote 🗳 that’s how they win, so register today and vote for real change
Encourage your friends and family to register to vote 🗳

I have lived in Southend-on-Sea for over 45 years and held non-executive and Board level positions at our hospital and a mental health trust and currently a residential and day care company.

Like many others I became disillusioned and felt let down by the Conservative party. For example: increasing taxation; failing to embrace the opportunities Brexit provided for our country; still tied to the ECHR; crime rising to unprecedented levels; demand rising and outcomes deteriorating in the NHS; a benefit system which is broken; uncontrolled immigration ……….

Personally, I don’t think the Labour Party has the solutions to these unfulfilled promises but could create a big state, big spend system. I believe REFORM UK has solutions to all these issues and will be honoured to work for and with the people of Southend West & Leigh to make our constituency and country a vibrant, successful and safe place to live and work.

THINK BIG, VOTE LITTLE

VOTE REFORM UK

Born and bred in Southend, Eva grew up attending local schools, including Southchurch High and is now a university student. She plays for a local grassroots football team and is an active member of a bouldering group.

Having grown up in the Green Party and joining as an official member at 14, she is passionate about ensuring that everyone, including all young people, in the area has access to education on how local politics works and are encouraged to participate on both a local and national scale.

It’s been a real privilege to serve my home, St Laurence ward, for the past four years. I’m standing again to keep working hard for residents and making sure your voices are heard.

Over my first term, I’ve focused on getting things done locally—improving roads and footpaths, upgrading our parks and play areas, and tackling issues like air quality and anti-social behaviour. I also helped stop the closure of Kent Elms Library, pushed for action on the sewage works, and brought national attention to the RAAC issues at Kingsdown School.

I’m proud to be a councillor who delivers real results, but there’s always more to do.

If re-elected, I’ll continue working on practical improvements like resurfacing pavements, expanding play facilities, protecting the X30 bus service, supporting fair parking solutions, investing in allotments, and standing up against unsuitable developments.

A record of action. A vote for results.

Gemma Deeney is the Green Party candidate for St Laurence.

Gemma lives in central Leigh. She grew up here and attended the local schools. A former primary school teacher of ten years and mother to three children, Gemma is concerned about air pollution, looking after nature & wildlife in our parks and our beaches, and increasing green spaces for all.

Gemma owns The Refill Room, a zero-waste, organic shop in central Leigh. Passionate about reducing waste and single-use plastic, The Refill Room promotes sustainability and well-being. Gemma believes community coming together is key to a greener, cleaner & safer town.

I am your Reform UK candidate for St Luke's Ward in the local elections taking place on Thursday 7th May.

I have lived in the Ward for the last 5 years, and have lived in the Southend area my entire life.

I am brand new to politics and just want to see Southend become a safer, nicer place to live.

I have a young family and am really passionate about improving our local area and supporting my neighbours.

I will represent our area against the destruction of the nearby Green Belt land. There is no chance this project will include the required infrastructure improvements. I will also stand against the HMOs that seem to be creeping further and further from the town centre, commonly bringing anti-social behaviour with them.

The Labour administration of Southend City Council has dragged Southend down for too long, with wasteful projects like 'Better Queensway' and zero support for residents and local businesses. Their only priority seems to be increasing their income and cutting the services they should be providing.

I have been unfortunate enough to experience the incompetence of this Council too many times. Even having a dropped kerb installed is a year long saga costing thousands of pounds.

All we need from our local Council is for them to charge as little tax as possible, and at least provide the very basic services they should be providing. We can't allow them to continue failing on both counts.

Together I am confident we can improve Southend. I will work tirelessly to do all I can for you.

Southend is broken. Southend needs Reform.

I’m standing to represent St Luke’s because local government should be about delivery, responsibility and protecting what matters to our community.

This election is about who will make practical decisions on housing, public services, neighbourhood safety and the future direction of our city. It is about steady leadership - not protest politics.

In Southend, Labour made five fully funded local pledges at the last election - and delivered all five. That record shows that when we make commitments locally, we keep them.

As a constituency resident and Digital Infrastructure Engineer, I believe in careful planning, responsible finances and public services that work for everyone. I want Southend to continue investing in our communities, protecting essential services and planning for long-term prosperity - not returning to short-term cuts or divisive politics.

With major changes ahead, including new regional powers and local government reorganisation, St Luke’s needs strong, sensible representation focused on stability, fairness and real-world delivery.

My commitment is simple:
Work hard. Protect local services. Deliver for St Luke’s.

Let’s keep building a city we can be proud of.

Tilly Hogrebe is the Green Party Candidate for St. Luke's.

As a long-term resident in St. Luke's, Tilly  is standing to offer a hopeful, practical alternative to politics as usual, arguing for a fairer, greener country where people have secure homes, reliable public services and a healthier environment.

Through her work with young people and her community involvement, she has seen how connection and inclusion make places safer and stronger. She supports decarbonising the economy, investing in green jobs and transport, bringing key services back into public hands, and creating a society where everyone’s basic needs are met while living within the planet’s limits.
If elected The Moon And Serpent Party will ignore the law and unilaterally open cannabis cafes around Southend.

I am standing for re-election as an Independent. I live in Thorpe Ward and have served on Southend City Council as an Independent for 22 years. I am staunchly and proudly independent. My politics are in the sensible centre ground. I am not interested in polarised, extreme or trendy politics. Over the the years I have worked with Conservative, Lib-Dem and Labour colleagues to deliver the best outcomes for Thorpe Ward. Thorpe Ward is a fantastic place and I think the best area in the city. This did not occur by accident, it is due to the hard work and dedication of Independent Councillors who have represented Thorpe Ward for 20 years. My son and I both own our own homes in Thorpe Ward, these are often our most valuable assetts. It is therefore logical that is my shared aspiration with residents to protect Thorpe Ward and all its great assetts. The most hotly contested issue is the Green Belt. The Independents will not agree to any overdevelopment, or any large development without the appropriate infrastructure incl Roads, Health, Schools, and Drainage. It is not possible for any party to gain an overall majority in this election. Once again the Independents will have the leverage and influence to get things done for Thorpe residents. Independent is the most powerful vote you can cast.

"The Lib Dem team are doing fantastic work bringing about real change for Leigh and its residents. I'm particularly impressed with all they are doing on the environment which is so important to protecting people's health and ensuring a future for the next generations.

"As a Councillor for West Leigh, I will aim to work hard to build on this change, ensuring residents' voices are heard and the issues which matter to them are given full consideration."

As a Southend resident, I care for our community. I cannot stand back and watch our city degrade into a dystopian 15 minute/ Smart City.  I do not support south church park becoming the "biggest beer garden in essex", the park is first a formost and place of tranquility, peace and for families.  I do not support the use of digital ID to access park facilities. I stand with protecting our enviroment, protect wildlife, mature trees and stop the irresponsible desposal of sewage in our sea. I will improve maintence of our local area and keep regular rubbish collection. I support local independent businesses. I stand for freedom, family and the Nation

Peter Walker is the Green Party Victoria Ward candidate.

A retired schoolteacher, Peter now spends much of his time as a Piano and Chess tutor, an avid cross-county cyclist, and as a member of the Southend Bach Choir.

Peter has long been involved in environmental conservation and the local Green Party, and he was also the Green candidate in Chalkwell in 2015, and for Blenheim in 2019.

Hi there, I'm so excited to introduce myself! My name is Jolene Hills and I'm a proud entrepreneur who has owned and operated multiple businesses in Southend. My mother is one of my biggest inspirations and she has shown me the business ropes having run many across Southend.  One of my recent ventures is the Cobham Hotel in Westcliff, which I acquired just before lockdown which, as you can imagine, was a difficult time.  However, we successfully got through it and I am proud to see the business thriving.

​

I have 2 children who I adore and who I want to grow up in a city that will give them the best chance of being happy and healthy.  As it happens, one of my daughters is studying politics so she will always be on hand if needed!  

​

As a born-and-bred Southend resident, I've always had a deep love for our city. However, I couldn't help but notice that there were areas for improvement. For example, I strongly believe that Southend Pier, one of the town's most iconic landmarks, needs more at the end of it.  Perhaps I am pointing out the obvious!

 

As a can-do person, I'm always eager to roll up my sleeves and tackle any challenge head-on.  I manage on the ground-floor, so I am used to dealing with all manner of people issues.  It is this experience I hope to bring to the residents of St Luke's.

​

I am thrilled to be getting more involved in my community to see what I can do to help. I believe that by working together, we can make Southend an even better place to live, work, and visit. So if you're looking for a passionate and dedicated partner to help you make a positive impact, look no further. I can't wait to see what we can accomplish together!

​

I am enormously excited to be the St Luke's Confelicity candidate and look forward to meeting residents throughout the year in my journey to become a Councillor at the next local elections.

Simon Gittus is the Green Party candidate for West Leigh.

Simon lives locally in Leigh-on-Sea and works as a designer, planning and shaping spaces in the retail and leisure sector. For the past year, he has been working closely with residents across West Leigh, alongside local Green councillors, taking up issues and pushing for solutions. He regularly attends Southend City Council meetings, holding decision-makers to account and raising concerns on behalf of the community.

Simon has been a clear voice on protecting our green belt, including Hadleigh Farmland and Bournes Green, improving bus services so they work for residents rather than private companies, and getting long-standing local issues sorted - finally getting the Marine Parade shelter fixed.

I am standing in West Shoebury because I believe local people deserve a councillor who will put residents first, speak plainly, and challenge poor decisions.

West Shoebury is a wonderful place to live, but too often local people feel ignored while overdevelopment, pressure on roads and services, and everyday issues like pavements, parking and cleanliness are not taken seriously enough.

If elected, I will work hard to be a strong local voice for West Shoebury — listening properly, asking difficult questions, and standing up for the people who live here rather than simply following a party line.

I believe local politics should be about common sense, honesty and accountability. That means supporting sensible development, protecting local character, improving the basics, and making sure residents are properly heard.

Like you I live in Westborough with my family, I know many of the issues which affect us locally and some of those you have raised with me and my campaign team. You want a clean, tidy, and safe local area to live in, so do I.

In Westborough you will have seen new pavements, extra street lighting and more trees. The Labour led administration is putting £10 Million each year into roads and pavements as part of the plan to make our city better.

We can only do that by providing value for money with the council tax you pay.  Our council tax is lower than Rochford and Castle Point which are run by the Conservatives.

We are increasing the pay of social care workers who provide care to the most vulnerable, giving them more security and ensuring we have staff to deliver these vital front-line services.

Building on the full-fibre rollout and our investment in the city's digital infrastructure, Southend is fast becoming a leading location for digital businesses, attracting more investment and highly skilled jobs and career opportunities to the city.

Achieving city status gives us the opportunity to take the next steps forward in building the city we want to become, and Westborough will be a part of that.

I hope you will consider supporting me to be our local councillor at this election.

- Councillor Kevin Robinson

Dear Resident

I am asking for your vote on May 7th to save the Westborough ward.

My name is Ben Aldridge and I am your Reform councillor candidate for Westborough.

As a former police officer with 15+ years’ service in community policing, Southend is broken. We cannot allow the current status quo to continue. 
I’m a father of two, employed locally and use to love Southend and be proud to live here. I want that feeling back and for my children to be proud of the place they grow up in. Not having to step over fly tipped rubbish on the walk to school. Not having to hope Dad avoids the pothole when we go out for a drive, as we cant afford to fix the car or buy a new tyre. Not having to hope the library is open as its Saturday and they don’t have the staff.

I am new to the political world and never felt I belonged but Reform aligned so strongly with my beliefs and the changing shift in UK politics I felt I had to do something. Not for me, but for the future generations.

The plans this city has to build on green belt land, which Reform will oppose and the amount of HMO’s I’ve seen just in this area, cannot continue. Our services are at breaking point and Southend is full.

Join me in the fight to bring hope back. Southend needs Reform.

Ben Aldridge
REFORM Councillor Candidate

Stephen Jordan is one of the Green Party candidates for Westborough.

Stephen is an HCPC-registered social worker and qualified as a social worker in 1988.

From November 2004 until March 2011 he worked full time as the Curriculum Leader for BA (Hons) Social Work at South Essex College. Stephen has worked as a lecturer for University of Essex since September 2014.

Stephen has been involved in the work of the local Green Party for nearly 20 years, holding several committee roles and first standing for election in 2006.

Over the years, Stephen has been the Green candidate in Milton (2006-2007), Victoria (2008-2010), St Luke's (2015), West Shoebury in 2016, and Westborough (2012 & 2019).

As a local resident, I see first-hand the everyday issues that go unnoticed and unresolved. It’s clear and come to my attention that some people have received little to no support when they’ve reached out to the council on certain issues. 
Residents have reported problems with roads, only for tickets to be closed without any action being taken. I’ve personally reported issues like fly-tipping and graffiti, and too often it feels like only selected jobs are actually completed.

No one in our community should feel forgotten or left to deal with things alone. I want to be someone you can rely on—someone you can simply say, “Ask Amy.” If I don’t have the answer, I will make sure I find it or connect you with someone who can help.

I want to be a strong voice for our ward and a true champion for our community.

As well as working to improve the area, I bring a solid background in supporting others—whether that’s help with employment issues, completing forms (such as Blue Badge applications), appealing parking fines, making complaints about goods and services, advising on pet care concerns, or supporting those affected by scams.

Together, we can make sure our community is heard, supported, and no longer overlooked.

I was born and raised in Southend on sea, where I grew up in Southchurch Village (Beautfort Street), with my mum, dad and 2 brothers. I went to school in Hamstel infant and Junior school and joined the King Edmund School.

I later moved to South Essex College to studding Sports BTech and remained to join the University of Essex to study sport science with the intention of becoming a P.E teacher.

Alongside my studies I worked within H.R. and despite completing my Degree I decided that I enjoyed working with people and helping them to progress in the workplace that I continues on this path. I am now taking a second degree, a business degree, which I hope to complete in the next 2 years.

My passion for people stems from my working with the public through my work with Southend Council, Southend Hospital, local Schools including shoebury, Southchurch High, Bournes Green, Thorpe Green Ways and Cecil and many local charities.

As part of my role in my organisation I am responsible for volunteering committee which helps in raising awareness and funding for a number of charities and organisations.

I look forward to helping and supporting the people of my home city in any way I can, whether that is just a chat or helping solve a problem a resident is struggling with.

I am thrilled to represent Thorpe and hope I can give back to the city that has done so much for me.
